name: pi-runcat
description: running cat working indicator extension for Pi.
readme: # pi-runcat Is a running cat🐱 for your `PI` loading bar (Yet another useless cat here..) ## pi install ```bash wget -P ~/.local/share/fonts "https://github.com/FredySandoval/pi-runcat/releases/download/v1.0.0/runcat.ttf" pi install npm:pi-runcat fc-cache -f # MacOS curl -L -o ~/Library/Fonts/runcat.ttf "https://github.com/FredySandoval/pi-runcat/releases/download/v1.0.0/runcat.ttf" # Then restart the app that needs the font. # If it still does not appear, you can refresh macOS font caches with: atsutil databases -removeUser ``` ## Manual Installation Font installation ```bash cp -r runcat.ttf ~/.local/share/fonts fc-cache -f ``` ## uninstall ```bash pi remove npm:pi-runcat rm -r ~/.local/share/fonts/runcat.ttf fc-cache -f ``` Inspired by [runcat-text](https://github.com/bzglve/runcat-text) Security: file hash: sha256:3c5be14dc51cd0d21b34cbd40fe147ff61480ce03655eb43571008975b395d94 [security check](https://www.virustotal.com/gui/file/3c5be14dc51cd0d21b34cbd40fe147ff61480ce03655eb43571008975b395d94/community)
